calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KRIS COX, ESQ. of Moscone Emblidge & Otis LLP, Tel No. 415-362-3599 appearing for Petitioner Granite Construction Company;
Atty. A. NICK SHAMIYEH, ESQ. of Shamiyeh - Shamieh, Tel No. 925-935-9401 appearing for Respondent The Pressure Grout Company.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PETITIONER GRANITE CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award is granted. The arbitrator did not exceed his powers by awarding attorney's fees to Granite or by failing to award pre-offer costs to PGC since both of those decisions were within the scope of issues submitted to the arbitrator. While arguably both decisions were contrary to the prevailing interpretations of the relevant statutes, an erroneous decision on a legal or factual issue is not a basis to correct or vacate an arbitration award unless the decision is outside the scope of the issues submitted to the arbitrator. (Moncharsh v. Helly & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1; Safari Associates v. Superior Court (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 1400) By citing and relying on Business and Professions Code 7108.5 in support of its claims, PGC made that statute's attorney's fees provision relevant to the arbitration and among the issues which the arbitrator was called upon to decide. The arbitrator's decision not to award pre-offer costs to PGC was within the scope of the costs issues submitted to him. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
